Capabilities

Seven verticals.
One integrated platform.

From reserve-based lending to offshore wind project finance, from EPCM programme management to independent technical advisory — Ascension Capital Partners covers the full transaction lifecycle.

01 · PRIVATE CREDIT
Structured Private Credit
Senior secured, mezzanine, unitranche, PIK, and RBL facilities across the full capital structure. DSCR-driven underwriting. Bifurcated and tranche structures.

07 · EPCM & Technical Advisory
Engineering, Procurement, Construction Management
We provide integrated EPCM advisory and independent technical advisory services for complex capital projects — bridging the gap between financial structuring and physical project delivery.
  • EPCM programme governance and stage-gate management
  • Owner's engineer and independent technical adviser (ITA) mandates
  • Construction and procurement risk assessment
  • Lender-side technical due diligence
  • Completion and commissioning oversight
  • EPC contract review and negotiation support
  • Cost overrun and schedule risk analysis
Approach

Principal-led.
Execution-first.

Every mandate is led personally at partner level. No sub-contracting. No Chinese walls. Direct access to institutional lenders, co-investors, and technical partners from day one.

Private Credit Project Finance Energy Transition Digital Infra O&G · Minerals M&A Advisory EPCM Technical Advisory
01
Mandate Qualification
Every opportunity assessed against our institutional threshold before commitment. Clear scope, clear economics, clear timeline — from day one.
02
Capital Structure Design
Full capital structure optimisation — debt quantum, tranche architecture, pricing, covenants, security, and fee waterfall built to institutional standard.
03
IC & Lender Syndication
Proprietary access to institutional lenders, infrastructure funds, family offices, and sovereign co-investors. IC memo, CIM, and management presentation — bulge-bracket quality.
04
Execution & Close
Term sheet through financial close — legal coordination, CP tracking, drawdown management, EPCM oversight, and post-close portfolio monitoring.

07 · EPCM & Technical Advisory

Engineering, Procurement,
Construction Management

We bridge financial structuring and physical project delivery — providing EPCM programme advisory, owner's engineer mandates, and independent technical advisory to lenders and developers alike.

E
Engineering
Technical design review, front-end engineering and design (FEED) oversight, engineering cost estimation, and scope verification against project budget.
P
Procurement
Vendor qualification, procurement risk assessment, long-lead equipment tracking, supply chain resilience, and contract terms review.
C
Construction
Construction progress monitoring, milestone certification, cost-to-complete analysis, and schedule risk assessment during build phase.
M
Management
Programme governance, stage-gate management, interface coordination, completion certification, and commissioning oversight for complex multi-contractor projects.
Technical Advisory Services

Independent Technical Adviser · Owner's Engineer

We act as independent technical advisers (ITA) for lenders and as owner's engineers for project developers — providing impartial technical assurance at every stage of the project lifecycle.

Lender Technical Due Diligence
Independent technical review for lenders and investors. Assessment of EPC contracts, engineering assumptions, cost estimates, schedule, and completion risk.
Owner's Engineer
Acting on behalf of project owners to manage EPC contractors, oversee design and construction, certify milestones, and protect the owner's technical interests.
Construction Risk Advisory
Cost overrun analysis, delay risk quantification, contractor performance assessment, and contingency adequacy review for complex infrastructure projects.
Completion Certification
Technical completion certificate issuance, commissioning oversight, performance testing witnessing, and final acceptance sign-off on behalf of lenders.
Operations & Maintenance Review
O&M contract review, operating cost benchmarking, availability and performance modelling, and asset life-cycle cost analysis.
Environmental & Social Advisory
Environmental and social impact assessment (ESIA) oversight, IFC Performance Standards compliance, and ESG monitoring and reporting for lender requirements.

Strategic Platform Memorandum · Version 2.0 · April 2026

Sustainable Global Institutional Origination to Alternatives Infrastructure Impact Fund

Ascension Capital Partners is a boutique alternatives advisory and capital formation platform providing end-to-end mandate delivery across energy infrastructure, renewables transition, private credit, M&A advisory, structured finance, and environmental commodities. The platform has assembled a $13.7BN+ Mandated Programme across 27 coded mandates spanning 5 geographic regions and 8 verticals — 100% self-originated, zero brokered processes.

$13.7BN+
Mandated Programme
27
Coded Mandates
8
Verticals
5
Regions
Section 2

The Infrastructure Supercycle

Global infrastructure investment requirements exceed $4.8 trillion annually through 2040. The convergence of energy transition mandates, AI-driven data centre buildout, critical minerals security, and ageing infrastructure replacement has created the largest capital formation opportunity in a generation. ACP is positioned at the intersection of these secular trends.

Dislocation 1
Tariff-Driven Fragmentation
WEF 2026: supply chains prioritising resilience over cost. National security premium on domestic infrastructure. US energy and DC mandates accelerate; friendshored infrastructure commands premium.
Dislocation 2
Energy-Compute Convergence
$350BN+ DC CapEx in 2026. 1GW+ campus demand. Grid bottleneck creates multi-year power deficit. Vertically integrated origination across fuels, power, and digital. ACP-US-EI-001 ($1.5BN) LoE executed.
Dislocation 3
Credit Bifurcation
Mid-market bridge (sub-$10M) structurally underserved. Banks withdrawing. RFR+600–900bps premium available. Lendsecure LLP $100M allocation secured. Countercyclical — rising rates increase deal flow.
Section 3

Competitive Moat — Six Pillars

ACP's competitive position rests on six reinforcing pillars that are individually defensible and collectively create a barrier to replication. The moat is not capital — it is institutional execution infrastructure and proprietary technology access.

1
Execution Playbook
60+ Financial Models · 8 Categories
Greenfield PF (10–20 day build), LBO/buyout, structured credit waterfall, carbon credit royalty NPV, hydrogen LCOH, 13-week restructuring cash flows. Compresses deal timelines from weeks to sub-72 hours. Capital partners gain IC-ready analytics on every ACP-originated mandate.
2
Proprietary Technology
4 Institutional Platforms
GTHERM Circular DC IP (sovereign cloud, NATO-aligned, 30–40% defence premium) · EMS/VPP Platforms (enspired, GridBeyond, Habitat Energy, Kaluza — 70–85% gross margin SaaS layer) · Hydrogen Risk & Finance Platform ($3.2BN SAM, hard-to-abate sectors) · Institutional Anchor Tokenisation Platform (dual distribution: institutional + retail).
3
Lender Profile Intelligence
10 Lender Categories Pre-Profiled
Institutional-grade lender profiles across commercial banks (JPM, Citi, MUFG, SMBC, ANZ), non-bank lenders (Ares, HPS, Blue Owl, Golub), ECAs, DFIs, sovereign wealth, and specialist vehicles — each profiled with LTV, leverage, tenor, pricing, covenant packages, and documentation standards.
4
ESG & Green Finance Full Stack
ICMA 2025 · EU Taxonomy · SFDR · GIIN IRIS+
ICMA Green Bond Principles 2025 (incl. Green Enabling Projects Guidance June 2024) · IFC Operating Principles for Impact Management (140+ signatories, $400BN+) · GIIN IRIS+ Core Metrics · EU Taxonomy DNSH assessment per tranche · SFDR Article 8/9 classification · SPO coordination (Sustainalytics/CICERO).
5
Standardised Document Library
26 Execution-Ready Templates
LOEs, NDA/NCNDs, co-origination agreements (ROFR mechanics), dual-party fee schedules, IC packs, Green Bond pro forma (ICMA GBP 2025), LMA/LSTA facility agreements, ECA application templates, and programme governance documents. Sub-72-hour LOE-to-execution is the institutional standard.
6
Technical Advisory Network
8 Pillars · Pre-Qualified
Roland Berger & Oaklin (strategic advisory) · Sustainable infrastructure specialists (APAC, EMEA, Americas) · GTHERM & EMS/VPP (technology) · Malika's & Waattup (EPCM/TDD) · Private debt/credit funds · ECM family offices (SFO/MFO/UHNW) · DCM global banks (JPM, Citi, MUFG, SMBC, ANZ) · Real asset sourcing (MENA, APAC, EU, US).

Part III

Five-Phase Platform Build

A five-phase evolution from deal originator to institutional alternatives platform. Each phase builds on the prior, creating compounding value and progressively harder-to-replicate positioning.

Phase 1 · 0–12M
Earn Track
3–5 deals closed. $75M+ deployed. 80%+ fee realisation. Work + arrangement fees.
$2–5M
ACP revenue
Phase 2 · 6–18M
Secure Allocation
ROFR + IC+10 + $300M programme allocation confirmed. Partner capital.
$4–8M p.a.
ACP revenue
Phase 3 · 12–24M ← NOW
Platform Hosting
Named origination partner. Co-branding. Direct LP introductions. Hosted infrastructure.
$8–16M p.a.
ACP revenue
Phase 4 · 12–24M
Syndication
Portfolio syndicated + securitised. $100M+ placed. Agency rating achieved.
Syndication fees
Recycled capital
Phase 5 · 18–36M
Fund I
$250–500M committed. Anchor LP. ILPA 3.0, GIPS, SFDR Art. 8/9. 1.5–2% + 20% carry.
$250M+
LP commitments
